methods.description: Fluorescence measurements were performed to assess the photosynthetic performance of cells under different light conditions using DUAL-PAM and Imaging-PAM devices.

steps.description: Kinetic measurements with the DUAL-PAM were performed with low light-acclimated cells in density of 10-µg Chl a ml −1 to which 4-mM NaHCO 3 was added.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare low light-acclimated cells with NaHCO3 for DUAL-PAM measurement

steps.description: The DUAL-PAM actinic light was set to similar proportions of red and blue lights.
steps.searchable_summary: configure DUAL-PAM actinic light with red and blue lights

steps.description: The cells were exposed to high light (1,700-µmol photons m −2 s −1 ) for 10 min, followed by a low light recovery phase (40-µmol photons m −2 s −1 ) for 18 min.
steps.searchable_summary: expose cells to high light followed by low light recovery

steps.description: The fluorescence value obtained with the first saturating light flash (8,000-µmol photons m −2 s −1 ) after the onset of the actinic light was used as reference (F m ) for the calculation of NPQ, using the Stern-Volmer equation NPQ = F m /F m − 1.
Kinetic and F v /F m measurements in the Imaging-PAM were performed with cell concentrations of similar Fo intensities (1-2-µg Chl a ml −1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: perform kinetic and Fv/Fm measurements with Imaging-PAM
inputs: cell sample with similar Fo intensities

steps.description: The lowest possible measuring light was used for determination of F o and a light flash of 2,400-µmol photons m −2 s −1 for determination of F m .
steps.searchable_summary: determine Fo and Fm using specific light conditions

steps.description: The NPQ capacity was determined by taking F m ' obtained by the last flash after 10-min exposure to ∼500-µmol photons m −2 s −1 of blue light.
steps.searchable_summary: determine NPQ capacity using Fm' after blue light exposure

methods.description: Functional absorption cross sections were measured to assess the efficiency of photosystem II using DUAL-PAM 100 and DCMU treatment.

steps.description: Low-light acclimated cells were adjusted to 10-µg Chl a ml −1 , and initial F o was determined.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare low-light acclimated cells and determine initial Fo

steps.description: The cells were treated with 10-µM DCMU to block electron flow between PSII and PSI and exposed to a short red-light flash of low intensity, inducing a rise in fluorescence, reaching F m .
steps.searchable_summary: treat cells with DCMU and expose to red-light flash

steps.description: To compensate the DCMU-induced rise in F o , the initial linear slope in fluorescence within the first 5 ms was prolonged to the initial F o value of the untreated cells.

Cross sections were calculated according to the procedure proposed by Tian et al. (2019) by extrapolation of the recorded fluorescence increase in DCMU-treated cells to the initial F o value of untreated cells.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cross sections by extrapolating fluorescence increase

steps.description: The extrapolated fluorescence curves were then normalized to values between 0 and 1 to calculate the reciprocal area above the curves, representing the functional absorption cross section of PSII.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize fluorescence curves and calculate reciprocal area

methods.description: Pigment analysis was conducted using HPLC to quantify pigment composition under different light and iron conditions.

steps.description: Low-light grown cells under iron-replete and iron-depleted conditions were harvested on 1.2-µm polycarbonate filters, frozen in liquid nitrogen, stored at -80°C, and the pigments were extracted in 81% methanol/9% 0.2-M ammonium acetate/10% ethyl acetate (v/v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: harvest and extract pigments from cells under different conditions

steps.description: Cells grown in iron-replete conditions were concentrated to 10-µg Chl a ml −1 and supplemented with 4-mM NaHCO 3 .
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate cells and supplement with NaHCO3

steps.description: They were then exposed to 2 h of high light, followed by a 30-min recovery period in the Dual-PAM as specified in section 'Fluorescence Measurements,' and pigments before high light, after 2 h of high light exposure, and after 30 min of recovery were harvested and extracted as described above.
steps.searchable_summary: expose cells to high light and extract pigments at different stages

steps.description: To monitor the xanthophyll cycle pigment amount during blocking translation of nuclear-encoded genes, pigments were also extracted from cells that were inoculated with 2-µg ml −1 cycloheximide prior to high light illumination and then exposed to the same light conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: extract pigments from cycloheximide-treated cells under high light

steps.description: The extracted pigments were analyzed in a LaChrom Elite HPLC system equipped with a Nucleosil 120-5 C18 column using a linear gradient of two eluents [90% MeOH/10% 0.5-M NH 4 Ac (v/v) and 90% MeOH/10% ethyl acetate (v/v)].
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pigments using HPLC with specific eluents

steps.description: The pigments were quantified using previously determined calibration factors.
